name: Jamtland County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the Jamtland arms

description: A square blue flag charged centrally with a silver moose (elk) attacked by a falcon above and a dog below, the historic emblem of Jamtland.
history: The flag is the heraldic banner of the arms of Jamtland County, derived from the medieval coat of arms of the historic province of Jamtland; Jamtland was Norwegian until ceded to Sweden in 1645.

features.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Jamtland provincial arms

features.role: silver moose (elk) of Jamtland, attacked by a falcon and a dog
features.symbolism: a silver moose passant, attacked from above by a falcon and from below by a dog, on a blue field; the historic emblem of the province of Jamtland, alluding to the wildlife of the region.
